data.title: A 'Supersonic Tsunami' Looms For The Stock Market, Expert Warns
data.description:
A series of rare volatility signals is flashing across Wall Street, with options activity, stock dispersion and correlation breakdowns converging in a pattern some analysts say has historically preceded major market shocks.

Several quantitative indicators closely tracked by hedge funds and institutional traders now signal mounting stress beneath the surface of a market that looks far calmer than its underlying dynamics suggest.

S&P 500 Dispersion Hits Extreme Levels

While the benchmark S&P 500 – as tracked by the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY) – has remained roughly flat over the past month, the average stock within the index has moved 10.8% during that period.

According to analysis from Nomura, the dispersion spread sits at the 99th percentile over the past 30 years.

In practical terms, individual stocks are experiencing sharp swings that offset each other at the index level, masking extreme volatility in single names.

Noel Smith, managing partner and chief investment officer at Convex Asset Management, said compressed correlations are amplifying risk dynamics.

The expert highlighted that the extreme rotation from growth to value, record hedge fund leverage, and heavy use of short-dated options have crushed correlations between stocks and increased dispersion, meaning individual names move sharply while the overall index remains stable.

That environment makes "dispersion trades" highly profitable.

These strategies typically involve going long volatility on individual stocks and short volatility on the broader index. If individual stocks swing wildly while the index remains relatively calm, that spread generates gains.

According to Smith, previous episodes when dispersion reached the 99th percentile often clustered around major market shocks.

In backtests, the S&P 500 posted “ugly” median returns two to three months after those signals.

Turbulence Signals Jump 10x

On Friday, Jordi Visser, head of AI Macro Nexus Research at 22V Research, released a note titled "Supersonic Tsunami: Why the Market's Stress Signals Are Flashing 10x More Than Normal."

"This month it has become clear that something big is happening underneath the market," Visser said.

"AI is causing uncertainty on the value of long-duration software assets, forcing a re-rating as investors question the disruption of AI across sectors," he added.

Visser's turbulence model triggers when three conditions occur simultaneously: a covariance shock, a low VIX and the S&P 500 trading above its 50-day moving average.

That combination signals hidden stress, where price action appears healthy but internal relationships destabilize.

From 2023 through 2025, a span of about 28 months, the model generated roughly 20 to 25 warning signals, less than one per month.

In the first six weeks of 2026 alone, Visser recorded 12 to 15 signals. That equates to eight to 10 per month, a roughly 10-fold increase in frequency.

"The market keeps experiencing stress, appearing to recover, then experiencing stress again, with each cycle potentially exhausting its resilience," Visser said.

Is The Supersonic Tsunami Already Here?

Visser tied the current regime shift to artificial intelligence. He referenced Elon Musk's description of AI as a "supersonic tsunami," arguing that the technology's speed and deflationary impact are reshaping sector leadership.

"AI speed, deflationary pressure and its reality is still doubted by most and positioning around the globe is still massively in growth and companies built on code which is now free and ubiquitous," he added.

He said a force moving this fast and powerful does not disrupt one sector but reshapes the entire landscape of winners and losers, creating cross-asset correlation shocks that destabilize risk models.

So far, the stress has remained concentrated in equities.

Yet, Visser indicated that credit markets have shown early signs of weakness over the past two weeks, raising concerns about potential deleveraging contagion.

For investors, the message is clear: the surface looks calm — but the pressure underneath is rising.

Photo: FOTOGRIN/Shutterstock

data.title: Congresswoman, xAI Stakeholder Invests In Tesla Robotics Rival Apptronik: Betting On Musk And Against Him?
data.description:
Rep. Lisa McClain (R-Mich.) recently disclosed owning a stake in privately held humanoid robotics company Apptronik. The trade comes after her investment in privately held xAI earlier this year.

McClain's trading activity, which can be tracked on the Benzinga Government Trades page, shows thousands of stock trades made in the last two years.

The recent disclosure, shared by Quiver Quantitative, shows McClain's spouse investing $50,000 to $100,000 in Apptronik on Jan. 12.

Why It Matters

Coverage of the trading activity of Congress members remains closely followed. Certain trades stand out for their timing, size, or because of a member's committee assignments.

Members of Congress and their spouses are able to invest in private companies as long as they disclose the investments similarly to their disclosures in stocks, options and cryptocurrencies.

McClain made headlines when it was disclosed that her spouse invested $100,000 to $250,000 in privately held xAI, the parent company of social media platform X and Elon Musk's AI ambitions. The company recently merged with SpaceX.

The investment in xAI was made days before the Pentagon agreed to deploy the Grok chatbot for military use.

In an interview with NewsNation, McClain was asked about the investment. McClain said she and her husband had no knowledge of the Pentagon deal, and it was not insider trading.

“Because if it was, we wouldn’t have bought 100,000 shares, we would’ve bought a heck of a lot more,” McClain said.

McClain laughed when asked the question. The comment on 100,000 shares may refer to the investment being for $100,000. Up to 100,000 shares of the privately traded company would likely be worth more than her disclosed investment.

“I can 100% assure you that we didn’t have the insider information.”

Apptronik's New Funding Round

Apptronik recently announced a $520 million Series A-X funding round, which is an extension of a $415 million oversubscribed round in 2025. This brings the total Series A commitment to over $935 million.

The AI-powered robotics company said the capital will be used to ramp up production of its Apollo humanoid robot and expand its global network of deployments. Apptronik said the investments could help the company accelerate its time to market, which comes as it faces competition from Chinese humanoid companies.

‍‍"Today's investment is a strong vote of confidence in our mission to deliver humanoid robots that are designed to work alongside humans, not just as tools but as trusted collaborators," Apptronik co-founder and CEO Jeff Cardenas said.

‍Apollo is designed to help in industries like logistics and manufacturing and expand into retail, healthcare and later on, into homes.

‍Lisa McClain's Investments

A report from Quiver Quantitative ranked McClain as the third top gainer among members of Congress for their investment portfolio gains in 2025. McClain had a gain of 37% in 2025, outpacing the 16.6% gain for the S&P 500.

McClain's trading history in 2025 included hundreds of stock purchases, including multiple purchases of Tesla stock.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: SaaS Apocalypse? AI Fears Spark Sector Selloff As Private Equity Titans Push Back
data.description:
Software-as-a-service (SaaS) and data-provider stocks saw sharp declines in the past two weeks amid market concerns that artificial intelligence will erode the relevance of the sector.

Some of the top private equity CEOs have addressed what many call the "SaaS apocalypse" as investors relay their concerns regarding the stock slump. Right after the initial sell-off, Anthropic released an upgraded version of its artificial intelligence software, Claude Opus 4.6.

Vista Equity Partners CEO Robert Smith noted in a video released this week that the market narrative around software has shifted "quickly and sharply."

‘Wrong, But Not Irrational’

"Investors are asking a reasonable question: what role does enterprise software play in a world where AI can do so much? We believe the narrative is wrong, but it's not irrational,” he said.

Smith believes enterprise software is evolving into something "more powerful," noting the market had similar concerns around cloud adoption in 2014. He pointed toward this being another area of value creation and opportunity for the sector.

The firm is understood to be hosting a webinar for clients and investors on Feb. 19 to address further concerns regarding the firm’s overall portfolio and positioning, according to a report from GuruFocus.

Thomas Bravo, Apollo Global

Meanwhile, Thoma Bravo's managing partner Holden Spaht told Bloomberg in an interview he believes AI is driving the next phase of innovation in software and sees this as a "compelling opportunity”. 

Spaht further noted that he doesn't view SaaS and AI as different things. 

"All of our software companies are using AI, they're selling AI, they're developing AI. To think that all software is the same, we think they are missing the mark a bit. When we compare that to the performance of our companies, which is very strong, we think this could be a really exceptional buying opportunity," Spaht said.

Thoma Bravo manages $181 billion in assets under management and holds more than 75 portfolio companies.

Other players in the space that have limited assets in software have addressed investor concerns during their recent earnings calls. 

Apollo Global Management's CEO Marc Rowan has called the investor reaction to software stocks "extreme."

Meanwhile, Ares Management CEO Michael Arougheti noted that the firm is "well prepared" to navigate market challenges including software-related risks.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Twilio Shows Durable Profitability Even With Fee Headwinds: Analyst
data.description:
Twilio Inc. (NYSE:TWLO) stock rose Friday after the cloud communications platform delivered a decisive fourth-quarter earnings beat and issued stronger-than-expected 2026 guidance.

Rosenblatt maintained a constructive stance on the stock, citing accelerating demand and growing traction in AI-driven communications.

Strong Q4 Beat Signals Demand Re-Acceleration

Analyst Catharine Trebnick maintained a Buy rating on Twilio and a $180 price forecast.

Trebnick said Twilio delivered a strong fourth-quarter beat, showing clear signs of re-accelerating demand and durable profitability as it shifts from a traditional CPaaS provider into an AI-era communications infrastructure platform.

Twilio grew revenue 14% year over year to about $1.36 billion, with broad-based strength across products and channels, the analyst noted.

Voice posted its best growth in three years, messaging trends remained solid, and self-serve and ISV revenue climbed more than 25%, she said.

Multi-product customer adoption also jumped 26%, while software add-ons such as Verify grew more than 20%, Trebnick said.

FY26 Outlook Comes In Ahead Of Expectations

The analyst noted that Twilio's outlook for fiscal 2026 came in ahead of expectations, with management guiding to roughly 12% revenue growth at the midpoint and about $1.05 billion in adjusted operating income and free cash flow, implying an operating margin near 18.5%.

She highlighted strong momentum in Voice AI, which surged more than 60% year over year, along with major customer wins that support Twilio's positioning as a foundational layer for AI-driven communications.

Profitability Holds Despite Margin Headwinds

Trebnick noted that profitability held up well despite margin pressure from rising A2P fees, as Twilio offset the headwind through platform efficiencies and cost discipline.

Operating margin expanded to 18.8%, and adjusted EPS reached $1.33, well above expectations, the analyst said.

Trebnick expects first-quarter revenue of $1.34 billion and EPS of $1.30.

TWLO Price Action: Twilio shares were up 1.68% at $112.26 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Tame CPI Brings Buyers After Margin Shakeout; Bitcoin-To-Zero Chorus Grows Louder
data.description:
Tame CPI

Please click here for an enlarged chart of Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 (NASDAQ:QQQ).SPDR Gold Trust

Note the following:

Japan

Prudent investors need to keep an eye on Japan due to funds having borrowed hundreds of billions of dollars in Japan and invested the money in U.S. securities, lately in AI stocks.  Japan's Prime Minister Takaichi indicated that the Bank of Japan (BOJ) may not hike rates in March, but a rate hike may happen later.  In our analysis, this is temporarily positive for the U.S. stock market, especially AI stocks.  

Stocks in Japan continue to outperform stocks in the U.S.  Please see yesterday's Morning Capsule.

Europe

Eurozone Q4 GDP came at 0.3% vs. 0.3% consensus.  GDP indicates strength in industrials, materials, and financials even though Europe is lagging in AI.  Stocks in Europe continue to outperform the U.S. 

Magnificent Seven Money Flows

Most portfolios are now heavily concentrated in the Mag 7 stocks.  For this reason, it is important to pay attention to early money flows in the Mag 7 stocks on a daily basis. 

In the early trade, money flows are negative in Meta Platforms Inc (NASDAQ:META).

In the early trade, money flows are positive in SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY) and Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ).

Momo Crowd And Smart Money In Stocks

Oil

OPEC+ is considering increasing oil production in April.

Bitcoin

For the day, tamer CPI is bringing in buying in bitcoin.  Once again proving that bitcoin is a speculative asset and not a hedge or store of value.

History shows that every time bitcoin falls below the average price of most holders (excluding bitcoin whales), the chorus of bitcoin going to zero gets louder.  In contrast, every time the price of bitcoin goes above the average price of most holders (excluding bitcoin whales), a chorus that bitcoin is going to $1M gets louder.

Just like bitcoin itself, the available data on bitcoin is manipulated by bitcoin whales.  For this reason, it is impossible to get accurate data.  In our analysis, the average price of most holders excluding bitcoin whales and Strategy (MSTR) is somewhere between $90,000 and $105,000.  The average price of Strategy is $76,038 per bitcoin.

In our analysis, an average bitcoin holder, excluding bitcoin whales and Strategy, is more likely to sell the rallies than buy the dip.  

We have shared with you all along when it was a contrary opinion that bitcoin is not digital gold, is not a store of value, is not a currency, and is not a hedge; bitcoin is simply a speculative asset that is manipulated by bitcoin whales.

Note that bitcoin whales are always acting like smart money does in stocks.

when bitcoin was above $120,000, we shared with you that bitcoin whales were selling bitcoin taking advantage of the strength generated by the enthusiasm of retail investors.  When bitcoin touched $60,000, bitcoin whales stepped in to buy to prop it up.  Bitcoin whales managed to get bitcoin over $70,000 when they sold again.  

What To Do Now

Consider continuing to hold good, very long term, existing positions and add tactical positions based on signals. 

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.
